docs.title: bahamut_ani_stat
docs.text: Toolkit for Bahamut ani gamer data

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python
  • uv

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Install the package and inspect the available CLI commands:

uvx --from bahamut_ani_stat bahamut-ani-stat --help

Common local development commands:

uv run bahamut-ani-stat parse get-premium-rate --print-output
uv run bahamut-ani-stat db create-tables sqlite:///anime.db
uv run bahamut-ani-stat db add-new-animes sqlite:///anime.db
uv run bahamut-ani-stat db add-animes-detail sqlite:///anime.db
uv run bahamut-ani-stat plot anime sqlite:///anime.db docs/assets/anime.html

The historical anime.db dataset is maintained on the accumulate-data branch. Daily data collection is currently driven outside this repository, so changes on main do not by themselves record new daily data.

docs.title: Step 1. Fork this repository to your GitHub
Step 2. Clone the repository from your GitHub
docs.text: git clone https://github.com/[YOUR GITHUB ACCOUNT]/bahamut_ani_stat.git

docs.title: Step 3. Add this repository to the remote in your local repository
docs.text:
git remote add upstream "https://github.com/Lee-W/bahamut_ani_stat"

You can pull the latest code in main branch through git pull upstream main afterward.

docs.title: Step 4. Check out a branch for your new feature
docs.text: git checkout -b [YOUR FEATURE]

docs.title: Step 5. Install prerequisite
docs.text:
# Install uv on macOS and Linux.
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Install uv on Windows.
powershell -ExecutionPolicy ByPass -c "irm https://astral.sh/uv/install.ps1 | iex"
  • uv
  • poethepoet: task runner installed through the development dependencies

docs.title: Step 6. Create your local Python virtual environment and install dependencies
docs.text:
uv sync --all-groups
uv run poe setup

docs.title: Step 7. Work on your new feature
docs.text:
Note that this project follows conventional-commit and bumps version based on it. Use the following command to commit your changes.

uv run poe commit

docs.title: Step 8. Run test cases
docs.text:
Make sure all test cases pass.

uv run poe test

docs.title: Step 9. Run test coverage
docs.text:
Check the test coverage and see where you can add test cases.

uv run poe test-cov

docs.title: Step 10. Reformat source code
docs.text:
Format your code through ruff.

uv run poe format

docs.title: Step 11. Run style check
docs.text:
Make sure your coding style passes all enforced linters.

uv run poe style

docs.title: Step 12. Run security check
docs.text:
Ensure the packages installed are secure, and no server vulnerability is introduced

uv run poe secure

docs.title: Step 13. Create a Pull Request and celebrate 🎉
Data Maintenance
docs.text: This project keeps historical Bahamut Anime data on the accumulate-data branch.

docs.title: Branches
docs.text:
main contains the application code, documentation, tests, and release metadata.

accumulate-data contains the generated data and plot assets used by GitHub Pages. The large anime.db file intentionally remains on that branch for now.

gh-pages contains the deployed static site generated by MkDocs.

docs.title: Daily Collection
docs.text:
Daily data collection is currently triggered outside this repository. There is no scheduled GitHub Actions workflow on main that records new daily data.

Until that process is moved into the repository, treat the external collector as the owner of:

  • updating anime.db on accumulate-data
  • committing generated data changes
  • pushing accumulate-data
  • triggering plot publication through the existing branch workflow

docs.title: Manual Plot Regeneration
docs.text:
Use the Trigger plot manually workflow when plot assets need to be regenerated from the current accumulate-data branch.

That workflow:

  1. checks out accumulate-data
  2. installs the project dependencies
  3. regenerates plot HTML and trend JSON files
  4. commits the changed docs/ assets if there are changes
  5. builds and publishes GitHub Pages

docs.title: Recovery Notes
docs.text:
If daily collection fails, do not rewrite accumulate-data history unless a corrupted commit has not been shared. Prefer a forward fix commit that restores or regenerates the missing data.

If generated plot assets are stale but anime.db is correct, rerun the manual plot workflow.

If anime.db itself is stale, rerun the external collector first, then regenerate plots.

docs.title: Release Check
docs.text:
Python package releases are tag driven. A version is published only after the corresponding tag exists on GitHub and the PyPI publish workflow succeeds.

Useful checks:

git ls-remote --tags origin
curl --max-time 10 --fail --silent --show-error https://pypi.org/pypi/bahamut-ani-stat/json

docs.title: Schema Maintenance
docs.text: The project currently creates tables directly from SQLAlchemy models and does not use a migration tool such as Alembic. Schema changes therefore need an explicit compatibility plan before they are merged.

docs.title: Before Changing Models
docs.text:
Check whether the change is additive or breaking.

Additive changes include new nullable columns, new tables, and new relationships that can be left empty for historical rows.

Breaking changes include renamed columns, removed columns, changed primary keys, changed foreign keys, non-null columns without defaults, and type changes that existing SQLite values cannot represent safely.

docs.title: Required Steps
docs.text:
For additive changes:

  1. update bahamut_ani_stat/db/models.py
  2. update the parser or CLI write path that fills the new fields
  3. add focused tests for the write path
  4. document how existing anime.db files behave before backfill

For breaking changes:

  1. write a one-off migration or backfill script
  2. test it on a copy of anime.db
  3. record the before and after schema
  4. update the data maintenance documentation with the recovery plan
  5. run the full test, style, docs, and security checks

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
At minimum, run:

uv run poe style
uv run poe test
uv run poe doc-build
uv run poe audit
uv run poe bandit

When a schema change touches generated plots, also regenerate plot assets from a copy of the data branch database before updating accumulate-data.
